title: Ricinocarpus spachianus
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus spachianus is a plant species classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, which includes spurge and poinsettia plants. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its leaves and flowers, which resemble those of other Euphorbiaceae species. Within the Euphorbiaceae family, Ricinocarpus spachianus is further classified into the subfamily Acalyphoideae and the tribe Ricinocarpeae. The tribe Ricinocarpeae is a distinct group within the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by the presence of a unique type of leaf margin. The exact classification of Ricinocarpus spachianus may also be subject to revision as new research and evidence emerge.

intro: Ricinocarpus spachianus, also known as the "Gardenia vine," is a woody vine native to the tropical forests of Madagascar and southern India. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Ricinocarpus spachianus include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-cancer activities. In traditional medicine, Ricinocarpus spachianus is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional Indian Ayurvedic and Chinese medicinal formulations.
